title: Bulk insert with on_conflict query: atomic counter increment
prompt: Write `MyApp.Analytics.record_page_view/1` that records a page view. Schema is a `PageStat` with `path` (unique) + `view_count` (default 0). The function should: (a) try to insert a new row with `view_count: 1`, (b) on conflict on `path`, increment the existing view_count by 1 using `on_conflict: from(p in PageStat, update: [set: [view_count: p.view_count + 1]])` + `conflict_target: :path`. Fully atomic via PostgreSQL's ON CONFLICT. Use `insert_all` (faster than `insert`). Write to `lib/my_app/analytics.ex`. (Define `PageStat` inline in the same file.)
tier_rationale: The dynamic `on_conflict` query form for an atomic counter increment is a non-obvious PostgreSQL pattern. Claude usually produces a racy get-then-increment pair.
research_citation: Ecto constraints-and-upserts hexdocs: use `on_conflict` with an update query for atomic counters
